title: Orange polenta cake
subtitle: Gluten-free almond cake with polenta for texture, fragrant with orange.
excerpt: Orange polenta cake is a moist, dense cake made with ground almonds and polenta instead of wheat flour. It is naturally gluten-free. The polenta gives a fine, slightly gritty bite; the ground almonds keep it rich and moist for days. Two whole oranges are boiled until very soft, then blended with the batter for a clean, direct citrus flavour.
sourceNotes: Almond and citrus cakes using ground almonds rather than flour are widespread across Mediterranean baking, particularly in Spanish and Italian traditions. The Sicilian variant using whole boiled oranges became well known in the English-speaking world partly through Claudia Roden's 'The Book of Jewish Food' (1997), which documented North African and Middle Eastern versions. Adding polenta for texture is a British refinement most associated with Nigel Slater and River Cafe-style cooking of the 1990s. This version follows those proportions with some adjustment for a slightly less dense result.

content.text: Orange polenta cake uses two whole oranges: boiled for two hours until very soft, then blended to a paste with skin, pith, and all. The pith loses its bitterness in the long simmer and becomes the main aromatic element of the cake. The polenta gives the crumb a slight, pleasurable graininess.
This cake is naturally gluten-free and dairy-free. It is dense and moist, not light and spongy, and it keeps well. The flavour improves after a day, once the orange has fully permeated the crumb.

Place {{orange}} in a small saucepan and cover with cold water. Bring to a boil, then reduce to a simmer. Cook for 2 hours, keeping the oranges fully submerged with a lid or plate. Top up with water as needed. Remove from the water and leave to cool for 20 minutes.
Heat the oven to 160°C fan (180°C conventional). Grease and line the base and sides of a 23 cm springform tin with baking paper.
Quarter the cooled oranges, remove any pips, and blend to a smooth paste with a stick blender or food processor. Set aside.
Beat {{eggs}} and {{caster-sugar}} together in a stand mixer or with a hand mixer for about 3 minutes until pale and slightly thickened.
Add the orange paste and mix briefly to combine. Fold in {{almond-flour}}, {{polenta}}, {{baking-powder}}, and {{sea-salt-fine}} with a spatula until smooth.
Pour into the prepared tin. Bake for 55 to 65 minutes until the surface is deeply golden and a skewer inserted in the centre comes out clean. The cake will feel firm when gently pressed.
Leave to cool in the tin for 15 minutes before unmoulding. Serve at room temperature, dusted with icing sugar if you like.

attrs.intro: The main risks are an oily batter from over-blended almonds and a sunken centre.

items.fix: The centre must feel firm before you remove it. At 55 minutes, press the centre gently; if it springs back, it is done. If it sinks under pressure, give it 10 more minutes.
items.cause: Underbaked, or oven door opened too soon.
items.symptom: Cake sunken in the centre after cooling.

items.fix: Ground almonds from the supermarket work well. If making your own almond flour, pulse briefly rather than grinding continuously.

attrs.heading: Troubleshooting
